action.reasoning: Observation: the DANN iteration failed late in validation due to a device mismatch inside cross-entropy: logits/labels were on CPU while the criterion's class weights were on CUDA. Analysis: this is an implementation bug, not evidence against the method. We should fix evaluation to move tensors to the criterion/device consistently, and also make training more robust by using separate weighted/unweighted criteria for class and domain losses. Since feature extraction is the expensive part, preserving the current approach and rerunning is the highest-value next step. Decision: patch train.py to fix device handling in evaluate_head and DANN training/eval, keep the ViT-L frozen-feature + DG head setup, run a moderate training job, smoke-test predict.py on val, and submit eval immediately if successful.
action.command: python train.py --model_name openai/clip-vit-large-patch14 --head_type dghead --use_flip --normalize --epochs 10 --batch_size 96 --num_workers 8 --hidden_dim 1024 --dropout 0.3 --lr 2e-3 --weight_decay 1e-4 --domain_loss_weight 0.2 --grl_lambda 0.5 && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output val_preds.json
